title: Mellow British Serrano Ham Pie And Mash
description: A comforting British dish featuring serrano ham in a pie and mash form, cooked in a cataplana vessel with a sour and shaggy texture, kosher-friendly and perfect for a cold evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the oil over medium heat and saute the onions and carrots until they are tender.
Add the diced serrano ham to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, chicken broth, apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper, and stir until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough and place it in a cataplana vessel, trimming the edges to fit.
Fill the dough-lined vessel with the ham and vegetable mixture, and cover with the remaining dough.
Crimp the edges to seal the pie and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Place the vessel in the oven and bake for 35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
While the pie is baking, boil the chopped potatoes in a large pot of salted water until they are tender.
Drain the potatoes and mash with a fork, adding a little bit of oil or broth if needed to achieve a creamy consistency.
Serve the pie with the mashed potatoes and enjoy.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
